mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 payroll level at 135,474k suggests ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with improving labor demand
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains below 100,000 persons per month for 3 consecutive months while S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5 percent, the Fed enters a tightening or restrictive stance that raises the cost of capital for equities and reduces net present value of future cash flows. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the trigger is activated.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.15% remains well below threshold, indicating accommodative stance
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 HOUST at 1,036k and RSAFS at 419,027M suggest ongoing demand recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the interaction signal and forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining more than 1 percent month-over-month signals deteriorating supply-side capacity utilization and potential demand weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the trigger activates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1.0 percent month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 INDPRO at 98.74 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceed 1.0% month-over-month while S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5 percent year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising real discount rates and compressing equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation accelerates in a low-rate environment.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 CPIAUCSL at 232.34 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% indicate low inflation and accommodative policy
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime where inflation rises ahead of Fed response
failure_condition: The interaction signal (high inflation + low rates) fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the trigger activates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 UNRATE at 7.5% reflects ongoing post-crisis labor market slack
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceed 0.3pp over 3 months while S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5 percent annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate profit expectations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when growth momentum is sustained.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2013 GDPC1 at 13,750.1B reflects early-cycle expansion
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth remains below 2.0% annualized while S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900,000 units annualized signals synchronized demand weakness across consumer and housing sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both conditions occur simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 900,000 units
evidence_refs: fact:May 2013 RSAFS at 419,027M and HOUST at 1,036k both above thresholds, indicating demand resilience
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or early-stage recession
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST below thresholds fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the interaction signal and forward returns drops below 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.
